title: Protocol to quantify and phenotype SARS-CoV-2-specific TÂ cell response using a rapid flow-cytometry-based whole blood assay
author: Mutavhatsindi, Riou
description: SummaryMonitoring antigen-specific TÂ cell frequency, function, and phenotype is essential to assess the host immune response to pathogens or novel vaccines. Here, we describe a rapid and simple exÂ vivo whole blood assay to detect and phenotype the SARS-CoV-2-specific TÂ cell response. We detail steps for whole blood stimulation with SARS-CoV-2 spike peptide and subsequent cell fixation and cryopreservation. We further describe thawing and cell staining steps for flow cytometry analysis. This approach minimizes sample manipulation and has a quick turnaround time.For complete details on the use and execution of this protocol, please refer to Riou etÂ al. (2021).

steps_raw.title: Before you begin
steps_raw.instruction: This protocol provides reagents and specific steps for quantifying and immunophenotyping theÂ SARS-CoV-2 spike-specific T-cell response from whole blood samples. Here, we present an 11-color antibody panel designed to identify IFN-Îł, TNF-Î±, and IL-2 production, memory differentiation markers (CD45RA and CD27), cytotoxic molecules (Granzyme-B and Perforin), and the inhibitory receptor, PD-1. Furthermore, blood stimulation can be performed with peptide pools fromÂ other pathogens (examples are given in this protocol using a Mycobacterium tuberculosis (Mtb) specific-peptide pool). For the cell staining steps, other antibodies targeting different markers of interest can be included.

steps_raw.title: Before you begin
steps_raw.instruction: Before starting, it is essential to check the configuration of your instrument to ensure compatibility with the reagents used in the proposed panel.

steps_raw.title: Cell staining for flow cytometry
steps_raw.instruction: This section describes the steps to detect and phenotype SARS-CoV-2 spike-specific TÂ cells. Here, we used an 11-color panel to assess cytokine production (IFN-Îł, TNF-Î±, and IL-2), memory differentiation (CD45RA and CD27), cytotoxic potential (Granzyme-B and Perforin), and the expression of the inhibitory receptor, PD-1.28.Prepare the fluorophore-conjugated antibody mix in a 1.5Â mL Eppendorf tube according to TableÂ 2. Start by adding the 1Ă eBioscience Permeabilization solution (see materials and equipment section) then the BD Horizon Brilliant Stain Buffer plus and then each antibody.TableÂ 2Fluorophore-conjugated antibody mixTargetFluorophoreVolume per stainVolume for 20 stains (+10% overage)IFN-ÎłAlexa 7000.2Â ÎŒL4.4Â ÎŒLGranzyme-BBV4210.3Â ÎŒL6.6Â ÎŒLTNF-Î±FITC0.4Â ÎŒL8.8Â ÎŒLPD1PE0.5Â ÎŒL11.0Â ÎŒLCD45RABV6050.5Â ÎŒL11.0Â ÎŒLCD4PE-Cy70.5Â ÎŒL11.0Â ÎŒLCD3BV7850.6Â ÎŒL13.2Â ÎŒLIL-2PE/Dazzle 5940.7Â ÎŒL15.4Â ÎŒLPerforinAPC0.9Â ÎŒL19.8Â ÎŒLCD27PE-Cy51.0Â ÎŒL22.0Â ÎŒLCD8BV5101.3Â ÎŒL28.6Â ÎŒLBD Brilliant Stain Buffer Plus10.0Â ÎŒL220Â ÎŒL1Ă eBioscience Permeabilization solution (q.s 50Â ÎŒL)33.1Â ÎŒL728.2Â ÎŒL29.Vortex the antibody mix.30.Centrifuge the antibody mix at 12,000Â rpm (âŒ8,500 rcf) for 4Â min in a mini centrifuge.31.Store at 4Â°C in the dark until use.CRITICAL: BD Brilliant Stain Buffer Plus reduces possible compensation artifacts when using multiple Brilliant Violet (BV) reagents in the same flow panel. This buffer is formulated to allow for reduced test volume (10Â ÎŒL/test), avoiding excessive dilution of the permeabilization buffer during staining.CRITICAL: Keep all antibodies and buffers on ice while preparing the antibody mix.CRITICAL: The centrifugation of the antibody staining mix (step 31) will pellet antibody aggregates that could lead to non-specific staining.CRITICAL: The panelâs performance depends on the sensitivity and setting of the cytometer and reagents. Failing to titrate reagents properly may result in off-scale events or a lack of optimal separation between the negative and positive populations. It is thus critical to properly calibrate the instrument and titrate reagents before the experiment (Maciorowski etÂ al., 2017).Note: To aid with the discrimination of positive events, one can set up fluorescent minus one (FMO) controls, where the marker of interest is omitted from the staining mix (Roederer, 2002). In this protocol, we strongly recommend using FMO controls for the PD-1 antibody.Note: Additionally, a âdumpâ channel can be added (for example CD14 and CD19) to exclude specific cell subsets from the analyses.32.Add 50Â ÎŒL of antibody mix to each well.33.Mix well by pipetting up and down (8â10 times), while avoiding creating excessive bubbles.34.Incubate for 45Â min at room temperature in the dark.35.Add 170Â ÎŒL of 1Ă eBioscience Permeabilization solution.36.Centrifuge at 500Â ĂÂ g for 4Â min at room temperature.37.Discard supernatant by flicking the plate (rapidly invert the plate over a waste container and gently tap the plate on a paper towel to remove excess fluid).38.Add 200Â ÎŒL of 1Ă eBioscience Permeabilization solution and mix well by pipetting up and down (8â10 times).39.Centrifuge at 500Â ĂÂ g for 4Â min at room temperature.40.Discard supernatant by flicking the plate.41.Add 100Â ÎŒL of 1% formaldehyde solution and mix well by pipetting up and down (8â10 times).42.Incubate for 15Â min at room temperature in the dark.43.Add 100Â ÎŒL of PBS.44.Centrifuge at 500Â ĂÂ g for 4Â min at room temperature.45.Resuspend in 160Â ÎŒL of PBS and transfer to prelabeled Microtiter tubes and store cells at 4Â°C protected from light until ready to acquire on the cytometer.FigureÂ 3The absence of viability dye in the presented protocol does not significantly affect the assayâs performance(A) Example of viability dye staining of lymphocytes (CD3 positive and CD3 negative cells).(B) Frequencies of dead cells in CD3 positive and CD3 negative lymphocytes (n=30). Bars represent medians.(C) Frequencies of Mtb-specific CD4 TÂ cells producing IFN-Îł, TNF-Î±, or IL-2 with or without exclusion of dead cellsÂ (n=30). P values are reported on top of the graph. Satistical comparisons were performed using the Wilcoxon matched-pairs signed rank test.Note: To ensure the procedure is simple and rapid with minimal perturbation of the samples (i.e., no blood dilution, no erythrocyte lysis prior to stimulation), the usage of a viability dye to differentiate live and dead cells was not included in this protocol. To ensure that this approach does not skew the detection of antigen-specific T-cells, whole blood assays set up to measure Mtb-specific CD4+ T-cell responses were performed in the presence of a viability dye (Riou etÂ al., 2020). Briefly, whole blood was stimulated for 5Â h with Mtb peptide, red blood cells were lysed, and live leucocytes were then stained with a viability dye. Cells were then fixed and cryopreserved until batch staining. Data were analyzed to define: 1) the extent of cell death in our assay, and 2) whether the absence of a viability dye affects the quantification of the antigen-specific T-cell response (FigureÂ 3). Our data show that cell death in the T-cell compartment was minimal (median: 0.19%), and the detection of cytokine-producing CD4 T-cells was comparable with or without the exclusion of dead cells. Thus, because blood is processed fresh and the stimulation time before cell fixation is short (5 h), the absence of a viability dye in the staining process does not significantly impact the quality of the results.

steps_raw.title: Preparation of flow cytometry compensation controls
Preparation of flow cytometry compensation controls
steps_raw.instruction: This section describes how to prepare single stain controls using commercial beads to calculate compensation and correct for spectral overlap between fluorophores.46.Prepare 14 Polystyrene tubes (5Â mL) labeled as describe in FigureÂ 4.FigureÂ 4Experimental setup of compensation tubes47.Add 100Â ÎŒL of 1Ă eBioscience Permeabilization solution at the bottom of each compensation tube.48.Add 15Â ÎŒL of anti-Rat or anti-Mouse Ig compensation beads at the bottom of the appropriate tubes.49.Add 1Â ÎŒL of antibodies to the designated tube.50.Vortex tubes gently.51.Incubate for 10Â min at room temperature in the dark.52.Add 2Â mL of PBS 2% FBS, vortex the tubes.53.Centrifuge at 500Â g for 10Â min at room temperature.54.Discard supernatant.55.Resuspend in 200Â ÎŒL 1% formaldehyde solution.56.Incubate for 15Â min.57.Add 2Â mL of PBS, vortex the tubes.58.Centrifuge at 500Â g for 10Â min at room temperature.59.Discard supernatant.60.Resuspend in 200Â ÎŒL of PBS 2% FBS and keep at 4Â°C protected from light until use.CRITICAL: Since compensation beads precipitate rapidly, vortex the beads thoroughly and regularly before adding them to the 5Â mL FACS tubes.CRITICAL: Since compensation control beads are coated with a species-specific antibody, it is important to choose beads that will bind the host species of the fluorochrome-conjugated antibody used. Based on the antibody panel used in this protocol, only one antibody (IL-2 PE dazzleâą 594) is produced in rat, with the remainder being produced in mouse.Pause point: We have not extensively tested saving the samples in the fridge overnight after staining. However, in the case that samples cannot be immediately acquired by the flow cytometer,Â the cells can be saved for 16â20Â h at 4Â°C, protected from light.

steps_raw.title: Quantification and statistical analysis
steps_raw.instruction: We used FlowJo v10.7.1 to analyze flow cytometry data. SARS-CoV-2 spike-specific TÂ cells were presented as the percentage of CD8+ or CD4+ TÂ cells that express IFN-Îł, TNF-Î± or IL-2. The gating strategy is shown in FigureÂ 5. A positive response was defined as any cytokine response that was at least twice the background of unstimulated cells and data are reported after background subtraction. To define the phenotype of SARS-CoV-2-specific TÂ cells, a cut-off of 30 events was used to ensure the accuracy of the measurements (FigureÂ 7). Data visualization and statistical analyses were performed using GraphPad Prism v9.1.0.FigureÂ 7Assessment of the minimal number of cells required to define the phenotype antigen-specific TÂ cells(A) By adjusting the time gate, we performed repeated (non-overlapping) measurements of CD27 expression on antigen-specific TÂ cells (ranging from 10 to 100 cells) to define the variability of the measurement.(B) Summary data showing the expression of CD27 on antigen-specific CD4 TÂ cells from repeated measurements of a defined number of antigen-specific CD4 TÂ cells (10â100). The number of measures done for each sub-group is indicated at the bottom of the graph (nÂ = 83 to nÂ = 9). The coefficients of variation are indicated at the top of the graph. Bars represent the mean with standard deviation.

As the quality of flow cytometry data heavily depends on several hardware factors (e.g., laser beam shape and location, choice and quality of optical filters, and sensitivity and resolution ofÂ photoelectron detection), careful optimization and calibration of the instrument are necessary.Â Optimized application settings have been described to obtain maximum resolution of cell populations and consistent results across experiments (Mair and Tyznik, 2019; Perfetto etÂ al., 2012).

All recipes in this section correspond to the volume needed to perform a whole blood assay on 10Â donor samples with two conditions (no stimulation and SARS-CoV-2 spike stimulation) using 400Â ÎŒL of blood per condition (i.e., a total of 20 tubes) and include a 10% overage.Cryopreservation solutionReagentFinal concentrationAmountDMSO10%2.2Â mLFetal bovine serum50%11Â mLRPMI 1640 Medium40%8.8Â mLTotalN/A22Â mLPrepare fresh for each experiment under sterile condition and keep at 4Â°C before use.1Ă eBioscience Fixation solutionReagentFinal concentrationAmounteBioscience Fixation/Permeabilization Concentrate (4Ă) Cat# 00-5523-0025%3.3Â mLeBioscience Fixation/Permeabilization Diluent Cat# 00-5523-0075%9.9Â mLTotalN/A13.2Â mLPrepare fresh for each experiment under sterile condition and keep at room temperature before use.1Ă eBioscience Permeabilization solutionReagentFinal concentrationAmounteBioscience Permeabilization Buffer (10Ă)Cat# 00-5523-0010%1Â mLSterile demineralized water90%9Â mLTotalN/A10Â mLPrepare fresh for each experiment under sterile condition and keep at room temperature before use.1% Formaldehyde solutionReagentFinal concentrationAmountFormaldehyde solution (16%)6.25%625Â ÎŒLPBS93.75%9.375Â mLTotalN/A10Â mLPrepare fresh for each experiment under sterile condition and keep at room temperature in the dark before use.
This study did not generate new unique reagents.
